evidence_text: Experienced developers in this study valued AI agents as a productivity boost.
citation: Huang et al., "Professional Software Developers Don’t Vibe, They Control"
source_excerpt: "Through field observations (N=13) and qualitative surveys (N=99), we find that while experienced developers value agents as a productivity boost"
note: This statement is reported as a study finding from the authors' observation and survey data.
caveat: This statement is reported as a study finding from the authors' observation and survey data.

evidence_text: Experienced developers retained their own agency in software design and implementation when using AI agents.
citation: Huang et al., "Professional Software Developers Don’t Vibe, They Control"
source_excerpt: "they retain their agency in software design and implementation"
note: The source presents this as a direct finding from the study population.
caveat: The source presents this as a direct finding from the study population.

evidence_text: The source attributes developers' retained control to their insistence on fundamental software quality attributes.
citation: Huang et al., "Professional Software Developers Don’t Vibe, They Control"
source_excerpt: "they retain their agency in software design and implementation out of insistence on fundamental software quality attributes"
note: This evidence links agent-use behavior to a stated reason, but it does not provide a detailed causal mechanism beyond that reported motivation.
caveat: This evidence links agent-use behavior to a stated reason, but it does not provide a detailed causal mechanism beyond that reported motivation.

evidence_text: Experienced developers employed control strategies for agent behavior by leveraging their own expertise.
citation: Huang et al., "Professional Software Developers Don’t Vibe, They Control"
source_excerpt: "employing strategies for controlling agent behavior leveraging their expertise"
note: The source directly states a how-element of use: developers control agent behavior through expert practice.
caveat: The source directly states a how-element of use: developers control agent behavior through expert practice.

evidence_text: The paper's most salient finding is that professional developers did not vibe code and instead carefully controlled agents through planning and supervision.
citation: Huang et al., "Professional Software Developers Don’t Vibe, They Control"
source_excerpt: "Our most salient finding is that, indeed, professional developers do not vibe code. Instead, they carefully control the agents through planning and supervision."
note: This is framed by the authors as their central reported finding.
caveat: This is framed by the authors as their central reported finding.

evidence_text: Experienced developers reported planning before implementing and validating all agentic outputs.
citation: Huang et al., "Professional Software Developers Don’t Vibe, They Control"
source_excerpt: "they plan before implementing and validate all agentic outputs (RQ2)"
note: This evidence states specific observed workflow controls rather than a general attitude.
caveat: This evidence states specific observed workflow controls rather than a general attitude.

evidence_text: Developers found AI agents suitable for well-described, straightforward tasks.
citation: Huang et al., "Professional Software Developers Don’t Vibe, They Control"
source_excerpt: "they find agents suitable for well-described, straightforward tasks"
note: This suitability claim is paired in the same finding with an explicit boundary against complex tasks, so the usefulness claim should not be detached from that limitation.
caveat: This suitability claim is paired in the same finding with an explicit boundary against complex tasks, so the usefulness claim should not be detached from that limitation.

evidence_text: Developers found AI agents not suitable for complex tasks.
citation: Huang et al., "Professional Software Developers Don’t Vibe, They Control"
source_excerpt: "they find agents suitable for well-described, straightforward tasks but not complex tasks (RQ3)"
note: This is a task-boundary finding. It matters to mechanism only in showing a limit on where agentic coding succeeds, not in explaining the internal cause of failure.
caveat: This is a task-boundary finding. It matters to mechanism only in showing a limit on where agentic coding succeeds, not in explaining the internal cause of failure.

evidence_text: Experienced developers felt overall positive about using agents when they believed they could complement the agents' limitations.
citation: Huang et al., "Professional Software Developers Don’t Vibe, They Control"
source_excerpt: "In addition, experienced developers feel overall positive about incorporating agents into software development given their confidence in complementing the agents’ limitations."
note: The positive sentiment is conditional in the source: it is tied to developers' confidence that they can compensate for agent limits.
caveat: The positive sentiment is conditional in the source: it is tied to developers' confidence that they can compensate for agent limits.

evidence_text: The study combined 13 field observations with a qualitative survey of 99 experienced developers.
citation: Huang et al., "Professional Software Developers Don’t Vibe, They Control"
source_excerpt: "We present a two-part study—13 field observations and a broader survey of 99 experienced developers"
note: This evidence describes the study design and sample sizes as presented in the paper.
caveat: This evidence describes the study design and sample sizes as presented in the paper.

evidence_text: The paper defined experienced developers as having at least three years of professional development experience.
citation: Huang et al., "Professional Software Developers Don’t Vibe, They Control"
source_excerpt: "We define experienced developers as those with at least three years of professional development experience."
note: This is the source's operational definition for the participant population.
caveat: This is the source's operational definition for the participant population.

evidence_text: The source cites prior human-study results showing one randomized trial where experienced open source maintainers were slowed by 19% when allowed to use AI, and one issue-tracker deployment where only 8% of invocations resulted in a merged pull request.
citation: Huang et al., "Professional Software Developers Don’t Vibe, They Control"
source_excerpt: "Through field observations (N=13) and qualitative surveys (N=99), we find that while experienced developers value agents as a productivity boost"
note: This statement is reported as a study finding from the authors' observation and survey data.
caveat: This statement is reported as a study finding from the authors' observation and survey data.
